_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record at registry/assessments/g8.json alongside oracle-coverage, g-series, f-efficiency, f2b-replicate, a5-llm, compression-census, define-op-census. Single-experiment record: g8 was FROZEN-but-UNRUN when the batched g-series assessment was written (its refusal to interpret g8 was correct then and is SUPERSEDED for g8 by this record); g8 has since been run (runner-9, Opus, 2026-07-10), mechanically verdicted FAIL, and cross-vendor GATE-A CONFIRMED. This record is the FABLE interpretive assessment — the last of the three completion pieces (verdict + audit + interpretation) for g8. Scope discipline: registry/assessments/g8.json ONLY — audit-status.jsonl NOT touched (update line reported to the coordinator for serial append); assumptions.jsonl NOT touched (one new ASM reported for registration); no verdict, experiment record, amendment, audit, log, or engine/instrument code modified; nsk1/g2d/knull and all other-line files untouched; no git run (branch index coordinator-owned).
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per the kern Fable/Opus execution split: verdict-gen already produced the mechanical verdict (pure function of frozen SAP + amendment overlay + chained log; FAIL, fired_rule_index 1) and the Codex/GPT-5.5 cross-vendor GATE-A audit CONFIRMED it (auditor-5, all 13 checks ok, endpoints independently recomputed float-for-float). What that FAIL MEANS for the correctness thesis is decided here and only here. Analyst role file .claude/agents/analyst.md; this record interprets, it does not grade, re-grade, or audit. Separation holds: run by Opus runner-9, graded by verdict-gen, audited cross-vendor by Codex auditor-5, interpreted by Fable — four distinct identities/vendors across the boundary.

SCOPE TENSION to carry, not a fault: the frozen record and verdict say rungs_measured [R0] ('no host model') and the envelope's licensing sentence is 'model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism)'. That licensing honestly covers the FRAGMENT and ROUND-TRIP legs (deterministic formalism properties). It CANNOT cover the LOCATION and F-VERIFICATION-OF-CANDIDATES legs, which involve a host LLM pinned only in the run config (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct rev a09a3545, temp 0). The location numbers below are therefore quoted host-indexed. The audit noted the no-host-pin-in-record as expected for R0; this is an interpretation-scope note, not an audit or verdict defect — and the FAIL does not hinge on it (the fragment bound fired independently).

measurement_refs.scope_note: Every number below is indexed to: kernel state = math-v0 (39 records, corpus pin 73e5d351) + kernel-v0 (8209cada) as frozen 2026-07-08; extraction layer = doc-gen4 pretty-signature layer at mathlib commit e3b73828 (fetched == pinned-sample commit, cross-checked fail-closed); sample = 1,000 random Mathlib declarations (corpus pin 9c2a3a88 via amendment 1); locator host = Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct rev a09a3545 at temperature 0 (run-config pin; location/F-verification legs only). Rungs measured: R0; scale language licensed: none. Coverage disclosure carried verbatim from the verdict: m0b 0.3542 @ molecules-v0, corpus/rung/kernel-state-indexed, NOT general coverage. Extrapolation envelope verbatim (binding): 'Model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism); the relevant axis is KERNEL size: verdicts on 54–10³ records re-checked at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring (G6/G7 re-run as regression). Literature anchor + licensing assumption: Formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale; only corpus composition shifts them.'

_surprise_note: No registered prior / claim.md exists for g8, so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Informally, and quotable only as planning-value context: the frozen record's own wilson_gate planning value for the fragment was expected_rate 0.03; the measured fragment rate is 0/1000 with Wilson 95% UB 0.0027 — more than an order of magnitude below the planning expectation and a factor ~3.7 below even the 1% viability floor. The location planning value was 0.92 (detectable alternative, printed per the P8 §1.6 decidability lint); measured 5/39 = 0.128, UB 0.2408. Both legs undershot their planning values decisively, in the direction design-math-sector §5 ('token-mass coverage… expectation: negligible mass [open]') and design-lean-route §2 ('a Lean type in CIC does not fit profile-M's closed FOL grammar at all') had flagged as the risk.

interpretation.headline: g8 is the first measured contact between the kernel's math sector and a wild formal corpus, and the answer is a decisive, cross-vendor-confirmed coverage FAIL wrapped around a clean machinery PASS. Coverage legs [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/g8.json, audit CONFIRMED]: the profile-M fragment captures 0 of 1,000 random Mathlib declarations at the doc-gen4 signature layer (fragment_wilson_ub 0.0027 vs the 1% viability gate — even the upper confidence bound sits 3.7x under the floor), and the pinned 7B locator places only 5 of the kernel's 39 math-v0 concepts in Mathlib top-5 with F-verification (12.8%; UB 0.2408 vs the 80% gate; host-indexed). Machinery legs [MEASURED: same verdict]: the F∘B round-trip reaches its fixed point 33/33 through the production renderer+parser, and bridge-F verified 7/139 live LLM candidates (5.04% >= the 1% near-zero-kill floor) — so the forward map, renderer, parser, and verification harness all WORK; there is simply almost nothing in range for them to work ON. The pre-registered consequence binds verbatim: 'below either bound ⇒ Metamath-only identity stands, Lean stays annotation-only'. The bridge-programme kill (near-zero F) did NOT fire. Net for the correctness thesis: the Lean-minting / formal-reconstruction leg is DEAD AT CURRENT SCOPE — kernel size 39 math concepts, signature-layer extraction, this locator — with a pre-registered re-entry (the envelope's own bulk-authoring regression re-run at 10⁴–10⁵ kernel records, plus the ntp-toolkit definiens-layer route), not dead as a formalism claim. The reject-reason decomposition is what licenses that scoping and no more: 720/1000 first-failures are unmapped-constant (vocabulary — the kernel-size axis), 259/1000 are extraction-layer censoring (ASM-0035, pre-declared), and 21/1000 are visible grammar/parse residue including 9 typeclass-binder rejects — the measured tip of a formalism wall whose thickness behind the vocabulary wall is UNMEASURED.

g8.verdict: FAIL (fired rule 1). Both coverage bounds fired independently: fragment_wilson_ub 0.0026987 <= 0.01 AND location_wilson_ub 0.2408374 <= 0.80. The third disjunct — f_verification_rate < 0.01, the pre-registered 'kills the bridge programme' clause — did NOT fire (0.0503597 >= 0.01). Per the frozen kill text this lands the FALLBACK, not the programme kill: 'Metamath-only identity stands, Lean stays annotation-only.'
g8.mechanical_numbers: n_mathlib_decls 1000; n_in_fragment 0 (fragment_rate 0.0, Wilson LB 0.0, UB 0.0026987); n_location_targets 39, n_location_top5 5 (location_rate 0.128205, LB 0.063819, UB 0.240837); n_roundtrip_fixed 33/33 (roundtrip_holds true); n_llm_candidates 139, n_f_verified 7 (f_verification_rate 0.0503597); instrument_valid true. Pre-registered count-only breakdowns (results-log/g8.jsonl seq 0): gate_reject_reasons — unmapped-constant 720, definiens-unavailable-at-layer 225, no-signature-at-layer 34, typeclass-binder 9, kind-not-statement-bearing 5, signature-shape 5, unsupported-token 1, parse 1 (sums to exactly 1000). location_breakdown — statement mode 3/30 located, anchor mode 2/9 located (the anchor mode is the pre-declared WEAKER instrument-authored oracle, ASM-0037); candidates: 84/139 unresolvable (LLM-proposed names absent from Mathlib's index — locator hallucination, not F failure), 11 F-rejected/mismatched, 0 extraction failures. roundtrip_breakdown — 33/33 probe statements, 0 sample mints (nothing was in fragment to mint). All cells independently recomputed by the cross-vendor audit.
g8.means_for_correctness_thesis: Three separable findings. (1) THE MACHINERY IS SOUND — the honest positive. The deterministic spine HS8 needed — forward map F (signaturePretty -> canonical pm-ast/1 with de Bruijn canonicalisation, caps, sort-check, vacuous-binder rejection), the renderer B, and F-verification of live LLM output — executed end-to-end with 0 internal parser errors, a 33/33 round-trip fixed point, and 7 genuinely F-verified candidates from a live model. The Law-3 shape (neural proposer <-> formal language <-> deterministic verifier) is again mechanically functional at R0, consistent with l3a/a5 on the oracle side. Scope honesty: the round-trip was exercised over the 33 probe statements ONLY (zero sample mints existed), so 'sound' means sound-on-probes + live-verification-works, not sound-on-Mathlib-minted-content — none exists. (2) THE COVERAGE IS ~ZERO, AND THE FAIL IS ABOUT REACH, NOT CORRECTNESS. 0/1000 in-fragment restated on the layer-visible slice: even excluding the 259 layer-censored declarations and the 5 non-statement-bearing kinds, 0/736 visible statement-bearing declarations minted. The kernel's 39-record Peano-core math sector (naturals, order, divisibility, basic sets/pairs) simply does not contain the constants random Mathlib talks about — Mathlib is 315k+ declarations spanning algebra through analysis [design-lean-route §1, measured 2026-07-07], and 72% of the sample died at the first gate on exactly that (unmapped-constant). (3) THE LOCATION LEG SAYS THE CROSSWALK IS ALSO NOT FREE. Even for the 39 concepts the kernel DOES have, a 7B locator found F-verifiable Mathlib homes for only 5 (3/30 by genuine content verification, 2/9 by the weaker anchor table), with 60% of its candidate names not existing in Mathlib at all. The planned bridge-annotation layer (design-lean-route follow-up 2) cannot currently be populated by a small LLM + F-verification alone at any useful rate; whether a frontier-model locator changes this is unmeasured and cheap to test. What the FAIL does NOT touch: the Metamath identity route (math-mm), profile-M's internal correctness story (39 records, validator-checked, adequacy checkable relative to the pinned basis — design-math-sector §5's epistemic win survives WITHIN the kernel's own records), and the F-verification mechanism itself.
g8.kernel_size_vs_formalism_limit: The tasked question: is 0/1000 a current-kernel-SIZE limitation or a deeper formalism limit? The measured decomposition supports SIZE-FIRST, FORMALISM-UNKNOWN-BEHIND-IT. For size: 720/1000 (72%) of rejects are unmapped-constant [MEASURED: results-log/g8.jsonl seq 0] — the failure class that bulk authoring directly attacks, and the frozen envelope itself names KERNEL SIZE as the relevant axis with a pre-registered re-check at 10⁴–10⁵ records. For the instrument share: 259/1000 (definiens-unavailable-at-layer 225 + no-signature-at-layer 34) are doc-gen4-layer censoring, pre-declared as a conservative-for-PASS / mildly-anti-conservative-for-this-FAIL-rule stipulation [STIPULATED: ASM-0035]; the ntp-toolkit route retires it for ~US$5–15 [design-lean-route §5, claimed]. For formalism: the VISIBLE residue is small (9 typeclass-binder + 5 signature-shape + 1 unsupported-token + 1 parse = 16/1000, plus 5 kind-not-statement-bearing) — but this is a FIRST-FAILURE histogram: a declaration rejected at unmapped-constant was never tested against the later gates, so the 720 upper-bounds the size-attributable share rather than establishing it, and profile-M's closed many-sorted-FOL grammar excludes typeclasses, dependent types, and universes BY DESIGN [design-math-sector §2.4, decision] while a large fraction of Mathlib is typeclass-structured. HONEST BOTTOM LINE: the data are fully consistent with 'the fragment widens as the kernel grows', and equally incapable of establishing it — behind the vocabulary wall there may be a thick formalism wall, and no measurement yet says which. That claim is registered as an EXTRAPOLATION (reported ASM-0039, load_bearing false, resolution path = the pre-registered bulk-authoring regression re-run of the g8 fragment gate + the ntp-toolkit definiens-layer re-run). NEVER a premise: no design may assume the fragment clears 1% at 10⁴–10⁵ records.
g8.kill_chain_read: DECISION (enacted by the frozen kill text, not by discretion): NF3 resolves to the pre-registered fallback — Metamath-only identity stands; Lean stays annotation-only (lean-ref/1 records, no urn:concept: minting from Lean) [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/g8.json, kill_criterion_verbatim + fired_rule_index 1]. g8 therefore SCOPES the Lean-minting sub-claim rather than killing the formal-reconstruction programme: (a) the sub-claim 'profile-M can mint a viable fragment of Mathlib NOW' is DEAD at current scope — kernel math-v0's 39 concepts, doc-gen4 signature layer, this sample, this locator — and decisively so (even the Wilson UPPER bound is 3.7x under the viability floor; this is not a near-miss); (b) the bridge-PROGRAMME kill (near-zero F-verification) did NOT fire — F verified 7/139 live candidates, so the verification machinery is alive and the pre-registered re-entry paths stay open; (c) the fallback is BINDING NOW: until a successor re-run clears the gate, no design, prereg premise, or pitch may treat Lean/Mathlib as a mintable identity source — Lean content enters only as lean-ref/1 annotations, exactly as docs/design-lean-route.md §2 already concluded on identity-theoretic grounds. g8 converts that design argument into a measured quantity: the honest ingestion boundary design-lean-route drew is where the numbers actually land. Nothing upstream auto-resolves: HS2/HS3 (Π soundness, semantics pin), g9 (authoring), and the Metamath leg are untouched; downstream, any 'kernel reconstructs formal mathematics' paper claim now has exactly one citable measured status: NOT at current kernel size (0/1000, UB 0.0027), machinery sound (33/33, F-verification live).
caveats: Kernel-state-indexed above all: the fragment gate measured math-v0 = 39 records + kernel-v0 as frozen 2026-07-08. The envelope's axis is kernel size and its re-check at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring is pre-registered and binding; this verdict says nothing about that future state in either direction (ASM-0039 stays EXTRAPOLATION).

Extraction-layer-indexed: doc-gen4 pretty-signature layer only. 259/1000 rejects are layer artifacts (ASM-0035); def-kind bodies are invisible at this layer, and per ASM-0035's own direction note the censoring is mildly anti-conservative for the fragment_wilson_ub<=0.01 FAIL rule — but the visible-slice restatement (0/736) shows the FAIL does not rest on the censored share. The ntp-toolkit route is the registered resolution.

First-failure attribution: gate_reject_reasons is a first-gate histogram, not an independent-causes decomposition. The 720 unmapped-constant rejects were never tested against typeclass/shape/caps gates; 'mostly a vocabulary problem' is therefore a bound-consistent reading, not a measured causal split.

Location and F-verification legs are host-indexed (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct rev a09a3545, temp 0, run-config pin): 12.8% located and 60% hallucinated candidate names are properties of THAT locator on THIS target set; the envelope's model-scale-free licensing does not cover these legs (see discrepancies). A stronger locator is a legitimate, cheap changed-input for a successor — but cannot change the fragment gate, which fired independently and is model-free.

Anchor-mode dilution (ASM-0037, pre-declared): 2 of the 5 located targets verified only against the instrument's own pinned anchor-name table (instrument-authored, oracle-leakage disclosed); genuine content-verified location is 3/30. Quoting 5/39 without this split overstates the strongest reading.

No-defeq-aliasing (ASM-0038) and orientation-swap-only equivalence (ASM-0036) make F strictly syntactic: pretty-printer defeq renderings (e.g. succ-as-+1) miss, a conservative undercount for gate and locale, pre-declared.
Round-trip soundness is probe-scoped: 33/33 probe statements, 0 sample mints. It certifies the renderer+parser machinery, not round-trip behaviour on any real Mathlib-minted content (none exists).

Sample-indexed: one 1,000-declaration random sample (pin 9c2a3a88) at one mathlib commit (e3b73828). Random sampling licenses the Wilson bound for THAT population snapshot; Mathlib composition drifts (daily rebuilds), so re-sampling accompanies any successor re-run.

R0 throughout; 1 rung; scale language: none. No statement here concerns any host model's end-task behaviour, and the programme bottom line is unchanged by g8 in either direction: zero audited end-task wins over the kernel-as-text null.

interpretation.cross_experiment_picture: g8 is a COVERAGE-BOUND NEGATIVE, and it lands exactly on the programme's most consistent measured pattern: the kernel's deterministic machinery keeps passing while the kernel's reach into wild content keeps measuring small. The series, all audited or verdicted under rails: m0b — token-mass coverage 0.3542 on the FRIENDLIEST natural-language corpus at molecules-v0, corpus/kernel-state-indexed [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/m0b.json]; compression-census — proposition-level ENGAGEMENT on that same corpus ~0.6%, two orders under token mass [registry/assessments/compression-census.json]; define-op-census — internal define-checkability 0.7710 on the SELF-AUTHORED minted substrate with the external-benchmark mapper leg untouched [registry/assessments/define-op-census.json]; l3a/a5 — engine exactness 100% raw, ON EVALS AUTHORED AGAINST THEIR OWN STORES [registry/assessments/oracle-coverage.json]; and now g8 — the sharpest point on the curve: against a fully wild, fully formal external corpus, fragment coverage is 0/1000 (UB 0.27%) at kernel size 39. The gradient is monotone and interpretable: coverage falls from ~1.0 (self-authored) through ~0.35 (friendliest NL corpus, token level) through ~0.006 (proposition engagement) to ~0.000 (random Mathlib, statement-mintability level). Inside the fortress everything is exact; the walls are real and they are close. g8 adds one genuinely new piece the NL-side experiments could not: in the formal sector the coverage boundary is ITEMISED — a named reject-reason histogram per declaration — so 'grow the kernel' has, for the first time, a measured, decomposable target (72% first-fail on vocabulary) instead of an undifferentiated mass. It also repeats the f2/a5-llm-family lesson from the other side: the LLM leg (location) failed mostly by hallucinating names (84/139 unresolvable), while the deterministic verifier correctly caught everything — the proposer is the weak seat, the verifier holds; that is Law 2/Law 3 behaving as designed even in a losing experiment.

questions_opened.question: Bound the formalism wall behind the vocabulary wall: re-analyse the existing details.jsonl under a counterfactual complete constant map — for each unmapped-constant reject, which LATER gate (typeclass-binder, caps, sort, shape) would fire next? Pure re-analysis of already-logged data (the kern re-analyse-before-GPU rule), phase:exploratory (quarantined, uncitable, cannot flip the verdict) — but it directs whether bulk authoring or grammar extension is the binding constraint BEFORE money is spent on either.

questions_opened.question: The location leg is host-indexed to a 7B model that hallucinated 60% of its candidate names. A frontier-model locator over the same frozen 39-target prompt pack + F-verification is Tier-0-cheap and decides whether the bridge-annotation crosswalk (design-lean-route follow-up 2) is blocked by locator capability or by genuine kernel/Mathlib mismatch. Requires its own prereg (new experiment id; g8 is closed).

Pre-registered fallback binds: Metamath-only identity stands, Lean stays annotation-only (lean-ref/1). Decisive at current kernel size; not a near-miss.

The proposer hallucinated (84/139 unresolvable names); the deterministic verifier caught everything.

ledger_updates.statement: First measured coverage of a wild FORMAL corpus, itemised: first-failure histogram = 72.0% unmapped-constant (kernel-vocabulary axis), 25.9% extraction-layer censoring (ASM-0035), ~2.1% visible grammar residue (incl. typeclass-binder 9/1000). Extends the measured coverage gradient: self-authored ~1.0 (l3a/a5) -> friendliest NL corpus 0.3542 token-mass (m0b) -> ~0.006 proposition engagement (compression-census) -> ~0.000 Mathlib statement-mintability (g8). 'Grow the kernel' now has a decomposed, named target in the formal sector; whether growth clears the 1% gate is EXTRAPOLATION (ASM-0039) pending the pre-registered bulk-authoring regression.
ledger_updates.scope: correctness thesis, formal-reconstruction leg; all figures index-bound to their own verdicts' envelopes

assumptions_resolved.id: ASM-0035 (signature-layer F; def-kinds count OUT of the fragment, STIPULATED, load-bearing)
assumptions_resolved.backing_ref: Honoured and carried as scope: 259/1000 rejects are layer artifacts, direction mildly anti-conservative for this FAIL rule per the register's own note — mitigated here by the visible-slice restatement (0/736 in-fragment among layer-visible statement-bearing declarations, derived by arithmetic from the seq-0 histogram). Resolution path unchanged: ntp-toolkit definiens leg (stub g8-ntp-toolkit-definiens-leg).
Honoured; carried as scope on the location numbers. Lapses only if a successor pre-registers a different admissible set.

assumptions_resolved.id: ASM-0037 (9 Primitive targets verify by pinned anchor table — weaker, instrument-authored oracle, STIPULATED, load-bearing)
assumptions_resolved.backing_ref: Honoured via per-mode reporting: this assessment quotes location split 3/30 content-verified vs 2/9 anchor-mode everywhere the 5/39 headline appears, per the register's disclosed-oracle-leakage rationale.

assumptions_resolved.id: ASM-0038 (no defeq aliasing in F — conservative undercount, STIPULATED, load-bearing)
assumptions_resolved.backing_ref: Honoured; conservative direction for both gate and locale, disclosed in-caveat. Cannot rescue the FAIL (an undercount only strengthens a sub-gate reading; the measured 0 is what it is on the pinned map).

assumptions_resolved.id: ASM-0039 (NEW — REPORTED for coordinator registration, NOT written here): 'the g8 fragment rate rises materially as the kernel's mapped-constant inventory grows' — EXTRAPOLATION, load_bearing false
assumptions_resolved.backing_ref: Motivating measured fact: 720/1000 first-failures are unmapped-constant (results-log/g8.jsonl seq 0). resolution_path: pre-registered bulk-authoring regression re-run of the g8 fragment gate at 10⁴–10⁵ kernel records (envelope verbatim) + ntp-toolkit definiens-layer re-score. NEVER a premise; first-failure attribution caveat carried in its rationale.

boundary_stops.item: Appending the fable_interpretive_assessed update line to registry/audit-status.jsonl
boundary_stops.reason: Shared append-only file under central custody; exact line reported to the coordinator for serial append, per tasking.
Tasking: register by REPORT, do not write the register; entry text supplied in assumptions_resolved.

boundary_stops.item: tools/kb/kb-sync-internal regeneration of internal_g8.json (ERR_KB_INTERNAL_STALE pre-existing from the run session)
boundary_stops.reason: kb-sync explicitly out of this task's scope; deferred by the run-log to the coordinator already.

boundary_stops.item: Designing/freezing the successor records (g8 bulk regression, ntp-toolkit leg, frontier-locator arm) and any profile-M grammar-extension response to the typeclass residue
boundary_stops.reason: Design + prereg work outside the analyst role. Interpretive input: run the $0 formalism-wall re-analysis (exploratory) BEFORE choosing between bulk authoring and grammar work; the ntp-toolkit leg is the cheapest instrument-honesty upgrade.
GIT RULE in tasking: no git; branch index coordinator-owned; changed-file list reported instead.

tree_impact: HS8/NF3 leaf CLOSED-AT-SCOPE: Lean minting is measurably non-viable at the current kernel size — Metamath-only identity stands and Lean is annotation-only (lean-ref/1), by pre-registered rule, cross-vendor confirmed, with the machinery (F, B, round-trip, live F-verification) measured sound and the bridge-programme kill explicitly NOT fired. The correctness thesis's formal-reconstruction leg narrows to: Metamath identity route + profile-M's internally-checkable 39 records (alive, unmeasured at breadth), with Lean re-entry gated on the pre-registered bulk-authoring regression (ASM-0039 stays EXTRAPOLATION until that runs). The G-series ledger after this record: g7 closed (kot-ast/2), g6 open (inconclusive), g8 closed-at-scope (this record), g2/g3/g9 frozen-awaiting-materials, g4/g5 draft — the semantics core (Π soundness, pin validity, authoring) remains the unmeasured critical path. Fable interpretive assessment is DONE for g8; with verdict + audit + this record, g8 is the programme's first fully-completed G-series experiment under the three-piece completion rule.
